title: tetragonia macrocarpa var. microcarpa
intro: Tetragonia macrocarpa var. microcarpa, also known as the New Zealand spinach or kōkihi, is a plant species that belongs to the Aizoaceae family and is native to New Zealand and Australia. Its leaves and stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves are often used in teas, infusions, and as a cooking green, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal preparation methods.
